Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Apr-17-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Robert O. Whyte, Esq. and Christina A. Dondero, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Niesar & Whyte LLP as successor in interest to Niesar Curls Bartling & Whyte LLP.
Robert O. Whyte, Esq. was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendant Vacaville Ford-Mercury, Inc. a Delaware Corporation or Bryan K. White, an Individual.
Amended default entered against defendants on February 14, 2008.
The Court granted plaintiff's request not to dismiss the Doe Defendants at today's hearing.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Niesar & Whyte LLP as successor in interest to Niesar Curls Bartling & Whyte LLP, and against the Defaulted Defendants Vacaville Ford-Mercury, Inc. a Delaware Corporation or Bryan K. White, an Individual as follows:

Damages/Principal - $84,632.45.
Prejudgment Interest - $3,130.65.
Costs $767.00, for a total of $88,530.10.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
